title: Adaptive and Agentic RAG Loops · Program Brief: Rag Agent Tool Authorization
summary: An agent decides whether and how often to retrieve, rewrites until satisfied, calls broad tools, stores its own answers as memory, and has no stop, budget, or trace contract. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the agent tool authorization increment in bounded adaptive retrieval state machine. Learners progress…
objectives: Separate retrieval from tools and enforce per-call schema, least privilege, tenant, data, egress, confirmation, timeout, and effect evidence.
Run bounded retrieve-read-critique-refine cycles with query lineage, evidence gain, duplicate detection, stop reasons, and total budget.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Rag Agent Tool Authorization, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded adaptive retrieval state machine.

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-agent-tool-authorization — Separate retrieval from tools and enforce per-call schema, least privilege, tenant, data, egress, confirmation, timeout, and effect evidence.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-agentic-adaptive-rag-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-agentic-adaptive-rag-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_agentic_adaptive_rag_4(retrieval_rounds: int = 2, maximum_rounds: int = 4, tool_calls: int = 1, maximum_tool_calls: int = 3, confidence: float = 0.82, authorization_checked: bool = True, stop_reason: str = "evidence-sufficient") -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if retrieval_rounds < 0 or retrieval_rounds > maximum_rounds:
        return False, retrieval_rounds, "retrieval-loop"
    if tool_calls < 0 or tool_calls > maximum_tool_calls:
        return False, tool_calls, "tool-budget"
    if not 0.0 <= confidence <= 1.0:
        return False, retrieval_rounds, "confidence-range"
    if not authorization_checked:
        return False, tool_calls, "tool-authorization"
    if stop_reason not in {"evidence-sufficient", "budget", "abstain"}:
        return False, retrieval_rounds, "stop-contract"
    assert retrieval_rounds <= maximum_rounds
    return True, retrieval_rounds, stop_reason

# Evidence: rag-agent-tool-authorization-18rbky
# Competency: rag-agent-tool-authorization.

    if tool_calls < 0 or tool_calls > maximum_tool_calls:

    if not 0.0 <= confidence <= 1.0:

    if not authorization_checked:

    if stop_reason not in {"evidence-sufficient", "budget", "abstain"}:
        return False, retrieval_rounds, "stop-contract"
    assert retrieval_rounds <= maximum_rounds
    return True, retrieval_rounds, stop_reason

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Agent Tool Authorization model · Theory Rag Agent Tool Authorization
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Agent Tool Authorization: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 6787 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound adaptive retrieval, decomposition, tool authority, confidence, stop reasons, and loop evidence; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate retrieval from tools and enforce per-call schema, least privilege, tenant, data, egress, confirmation, timeout, and effect evidence.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Agent Tool Authorization model · Theory Rag Agent Tool Authorization.
steps.why: Retrieving Rag Agent Tool Authorization through the concrete bounded adaptive retrieval state machine case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Agent Tool Authorization model · Theory Rag Agent Tool Authorization, keep Rag Agent Tool Authorization connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate retrieval from tools and enforce per-call schema, least privilege, tenant, data, egress, confirmation, timeout, and effect evidence.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Agent Tool Authorization model · Theory Rag Agent Tool Authorization, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Agent Tool Authorization precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Agent Tool Authorization model · Theory Rag Agent Tool Authorization, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 6787 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound adaptive retrieval, decomposition, tool authority, confidence, stop reasons, and loop evidence; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate retrieval from tools and enforce per-call schema, least privilege, tenant, data, egress, confirmation, timeout, and effect evidence.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Agent Tool Authorization model · Theory Rag Agent Tool Authorization, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: A retrieval agent needs broad tool access to answer comprehensively.
